dataStartDate.description: Start date of the data in the reports belonging to this template.

dataEndDate.description: End date of the data in the reports belonging to this template.

id.description: A unique identifier for the person.

emails.description: The email addresses of the person.

type.description:
The type of phone number.
 * `work` - Work phone number of the person.
 * `work_extension` - Work extension of the person. For the Webex Calling person, the value will have a routing prefix along with the extension.
 * `mobile` - Mobile number of the person.
 * `fax` - FAX number of the person.

value.description: The phone number.

extension.description: The Webex Calling extension for the person. Only applies to a person with a Webex Calling license.

locationId.description: The ID of the location for this person retrieved from BroadCloud.

displayName.description: The full name of the person.

nickName.description: The nickname of the person if configured. If no nickname is configured for the person, this field will not be present.

firstName.description: The first name of the person.

lastName.description: The last name of the person.

avatar.description: The URL to the person's avatar in PNG format.

orgId.description: The ID of the organization to which this person belongs.

roles.description: An array of role strings representing the roles to which this admin user belongs.

licenses.description: An array of license strings allocated to this person.

department.description: The business department the user belongs to.

type.description: The type of address.

postalCode.description: The user's postal or zip code.

created.description: The date and time the person was created.

lastModified.description: The date and time the person was last changed.

timezone.description: The time zone of the person if configured. If no timezone is configured on the account, this field will not be present.

lastActivity.description: The date and time of the person's last activity within Webex. This will only be returned for people within your organization or an organization you manage. Presence information will not be shown if the authenticated user has [disabled status sharing](https://help.webex.com/nkzs6wl/).

siteUrls.description: One or several site names where this user has a role (host or attendee).

type.description:
The type of SIP address.
 * `personal-room` - Personal room address.
 * `enterprise` - Enterprise address.
 * `cloud-calling` - Cloud calling address.

value.description: The SIP address.

primary.description: Primary SIP address of the person.

xmppFederationJid.description: Identifier for intra-domain federation with other XMPP based messenger systems.

status.description:
The current presence status of the person. This will only be returned for people within your organization or an organization you manage. Presence information will not be shown if the authenticated user has [disabled status sharing](https://help.webex.com/nkzs6wl/). Presence status is different from Control Hub's "Last Service Access Time" which indicates the last time an oAuth token was issued for this user.
 * `active` - Active within the last 10 minutes.
 * `call` - The user is in a call.
 * `DoNotDisturb` - The user has manually set their status to "Do Not Disturb".
 * `inactive` - Last activity occurred more than 10 minutes ago.
 * `meeting` - The user is in a meeting.
 * `OutOfOffice` - The user or a Hybrid Calendar service has indicated that they are "Out of Office".
 * `pending` - The user has never logged in; a status cannot be determined.
 * `presenting` - The user is sharing content.
 * `unknown` - The user’s status could not be determined.

invitePending.description:
Whether or not an invite is pending for the user to complete account activation. This property is only returned if the authenticated user is an admin user for the person's organization.
 * `true` - The person has been invited to Webex but has not created an account.
 * `false` - An invite is not pending for this person.

loginEnabled.description:
Whether or not the user is allowed to use Webex. This property is only returned if the authenticated user is an admin user for the person's organization.
 * `true` - The person _can_ log into Webex.
 * `false` - The person _cannot_ log into Webex.

type.description:
The type of person account, such as person or bot.
 * `person` - Account belongs to a person.
 * `bot` - Account is a bot user.
 * `appuser` - Account is a [guest user](/docs/guest-issuer).

notFoundIds.description: An array of person IDs that could not be found.

recordingId.description: A unique identifier for the recording.

timeRecorded.description: The date and time the recording started in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format. the time is the record button was clicked in the meeting.

siteUrl.description: Site URL for the recording.

hostEmail.description: Email address for the meeting host.

viewCount.description: The number of times the recording was viewed.

downloadCount.description: The number of times the recording was downloaded.

name.description: The name of the person who accessed the recording.

email.description: The email address of the person who accessed the recording.

accessTime.description: The date and time the recording was accessed in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

viewed.description: Whether or not the recording was viewed by the person.

downloaded.description: Whether or not the recording was downloaded by the person.

archiveId.description: A unique identifier for the meeting archive summary.

createTime.description: The date and time in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format that when the archive was created by the system.

archiveId.description: A unique identifier for the meeting archive summary.

start.description: Start time for meeting in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

end.description: End time for a meeting in ISO 8601 compliant format.

hostDisplayName.description: Display name for the meeting host.

hostEmail.description: Email address for the meeting host.

chats.description: The chats of the meeting archive.

polls.description: The polls of the meeting archive.

qas.description: Meeting meeting archive's Q and A.

systemInfos.description: The system Information of the meeting archive, which can be only supported when serviceType is `SupportCenter`.

displayName.description: Display name for the meeting participant.

leftTime.description: The time the participant left the meeting.

email.description: Email address for the meeting participant.

type.description: Whether the type of the chat is private, public or group. Private chat is for the 1:1 chat. Public chat is for the message which is sent to all the people in the meeting. Group chat is for the message which is sent to a small group of people, like a message to the "host and presenter".

senderName.description: Display name for the sender of the chat snippet.

chatTime.description: Chat time for the chat snippet in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

target.description: Information of the receivers of the chat snippet.

text.description: The text of the chat snippet.

endTime.description: The date and time the poll ended in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

votedUserCount.description: The number of voters among users.

content.description: The content of the meeting archive poll;

choiceCount.description: The number of choices in the questions.

type.description: The type of the question.

answerSummary.description: The answer summary of the archive poll.

correlationId.description: An internal ID that is associated with each join.

displayName.description: Display name for the meeting participant.

email.description: Email address for the meeting participant.

displayName.description: Display name for the poll respondent.

answers.description: An array of answers to the question.

type.description: Whether the type of the Q and A is private, public, or group. Private Q and A is for the 1:1 chat. Public Q and A are for the message which is sent to all the people in the meeting. Group Q and A are for the message which is sent to a small group of people, like a Q and A to "host and presenter".

displayName.description: The email of the user who asked the question.

questionTime.description: The date and time the question was created in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

target.description: Information of the user who asked the question.

id.description: A unique identifier for the recording.

meetingId.description: Unique identifier for the recording's ended meeting instance.

scheduledMeetingId.description: Unique identifier for the recording's scheduled meeting instance.

meetingSeriesId.description: Unique identifier for the recording's meeting series.

createTime.description: The date and time recording was created in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format. Please note that it's not the time the record button was clicked in meeting but the time the recording file was generated offline.

timeRecorded.description: The date and time recording started in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format. It indicates when the record button was clicked in the meeting.

siteUrl.description: Site URL for the recording.

downloadUrl.description: The download link for recording. This attribute is not available if **Prevent downloading** has been turned on for the recording being requested. The **Prevent downloading** option can be viewed and set by a site admin on [Control Hub](https://help.webex.com/en-us/article/sxdj4ab/Manage-Security-for-a-Cisco-Webex-Site-in-Cisco-Webex-Control-Hub).

format.description:
* `MP4` - Recording file format is MP4.
 * `ARF` - Recording file format is ARF, a proprietary Webex recording format.
 * `UPLOADED` - The recording file is uploaded manually.

serviceType.description:
The service type for the recording.
 * `MeetingCenter` - The service type for the recording is meeting.
 * `EventCenter` - The service type for the recording is the event.
 * `TrainingCenter` - The service type for the recording is the training session.
 * `SupportCenter` - The service type for the recording is the support meeting.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the recording, in seconds.

sizeBytes.description: The size of the recording file, in bytes.

shareToMe.description: Whether or not the recording has been shared to the current user. This attribute is hidden if the user calling the API is a Compliance Officer and `hostEmail` is not specified.

integrationTags.description: External keys of the parent meeting created by an integration application. They could be Zendesk ticket IDs, Jira IDs, Salesforce Opportunity IDs, etc. The integration application queries recordings by a key in its own domain.

status.description:
* `available` - Recording is available.
 * `deleted` - Recording has been moved into recycle bin.
 * `purged` - Recording has been purged from the recycle bin. Only applies if the user calling the API is a Compliance Officer and `meetingId` is specified.

sizeBytes.description: The size of the recording file in bytes.

shareToMe.description: Whether or not the recording has been shared to the current user.

recordingDownloadLink.description: The download link for recording MP4/ARF file without HTML page rendering in browser or HTTP redirect. Expires 3 hours after the API request.

audioDownloadLink.description: The download link for recording audio file without HTML page rendering in browser or HTTP redirect. This attribute is not available if **Prevent Downloading** has been turned on for the recording being requested. Expires 3 hours after the API request.

transcriptDownloadLink.description: The download link for recording transcript file without HTML page rendering in browser or HTTP redirect. This attribute is not available if **Prevent Downloading** has been turned on for the recording being requested. Expires 3 hours after the API request.

expiration.description: The date and time when `recordingDownloadLink`, `audioDownloadLink`, and `transcriptDownloadLink` expire in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

temporaryDirectDownloadLinks.description: The download links for MP4/ARF, audio, and transcript of the recording without HTML page rendering in browser or HTTP redirect. This attribute is not available if the user is not a [Compliance Officer](/docs/compliance#compliance) and **Prevent Downloading** has been turned on for the recording being requested. The Prevent Downloading option can be viewed and set on page when editing a recording. Note that there are various products in [Webex Suite](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/conferencing/product_comparison.html) such as "Webex Meetings", "Webex Training" and "Webex Events".

integrationTags.description: External keys of the parent meeting created by an integration application. The key can be Zendesk ticket IDs, Jira IDs, Salesforce Opportunity IDs, etc. The integration application queries recordings by a key in its own domain.

status.description:
* `available` - Recording is available.
 * `deleted` - Recording has been moved to the recycle bin.
 * `purged` - Recording has been purged from the recycle bin. Please note that only a compliance officer can access recordings with a `purged` status.

reason.description: Reason for deleting a recording. Only required when a Compliance Officer is operating on another user's recording.

comment.description: Compliance Officer's explanation for deleting a recording. The comment can be a maximum of 255 characters long.

recordingIds.description: Recording IDs for removing recordings into the recycle bin in batch. Please note that all the recording IDs should belong to the site of `siteUrl` or the user's preferred site if `siteUrl` is not specified.

siteUrl.description: URL of the Webex site from which the API deletes recordings. If not specified, the API deletes recordings from the user's preferred site. All available Webex sites and preferred sites of a user can be retrieved by the [Get Site List](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/get-site-list) API.

restoreAll.description: If not specified or `false`, restores the recordings specified by `recordingIds`. If `true`, restores all recordings from the recycle bin.

recordingIds.description: Recording IDs for recovering recordings from the recycle bin in batch. Note that all the recording IDs should belong to the site of `siteUrl` or the user's preferred site if `siteUrl` is not specified.

siteUrl.description: URL of the Webex site from which the API restores recordings. If not specified, the API restores recordings from a user's preferred site. All available Webex sites and preferred sites of a user can be retrieved by [Get Site List](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/get-site-list) API.

purgeAll.description: If not specified or `false`, purges the recordings specified by `recordingIds`. If `true`, purges all recordings from the recycle bin.

recordingIds.description: Recording IDs for purging recordings from the recycle bin in batch. Note that all the recording IDs should belong to the site of `siteUrl` or the user's preferred site if `siteUrl` is not specified.

siteUrl.description: URL of the Webex site from which the API purges recordings. If not specified, the API purges recordings from user's preferred site. All available Webex sites and preferred sites of the user can be retrieved by [Get Site List](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/get-site-list) API.

id.description: A unique identifier for the webhook.

name.description: A user-friendly name for the webhook.

targetUrl.description: URL that receives POST requests for each event.

resource.description:
Resource type for the webhook. Creating a webhook requires 'read' scope on the resource the webhook is for.
 * `attachmentActions` - [Attachment Actions](/docs/api/v1/attachment-actions) resource.
 * `dataSources` - [data sources](/docs/api/v1/data-sources) resource.
 * `memberships` - [Memberships](/docs/api/v1/memberships) resource.
 * `messages` - [Messages](/docs/api/v1/messages) resource.
 * `rooms` - [Rooms](/docs/api/v1/rooms) resource.
 * `meetings` - [Meetings](/docs/api/v1/meetings) resource.
 * `recordings` - [Recordings](/docs/api/v1/recordings) resource.
 * `convergedRecordings` - [CallRecordings](/docs/api/v1/converged-recordings) resource.
 * `meetingParticipants` - [Meeting Participants](/docs/api/v1/meeting-participants) resource.
 * `meetingTranscripts` - [Meeting Transcripts](/docs/api/v1/meeting-transcripts) resource.
 * `uc_counters` - Performance counter for a dedicated instance.
 * `serviceApp` - Service App authorization notification.
 * `adminBatchJobs` - Admin Batch Jobs notification.

event.description:
Event type for the webhook.
 * `created` - An object was created.
 * `updated` - An object was updated.
 * `deleted` - An object was deleted.
 * `started` - A meeting was started.
 * `ended` - A meeting was ended.
 * `joined` - A participant joined.
 * `left` - A participant left.
 * `migrated` - A room was migrated to a different geography. The roomId has changed.
 * `authorized` - A Service App was authorized.
 * `deauthorized` - A Service App was deauthorized.
 * `statusChanged` - Status of admin batch job was changed.

filter.description: Filter that defines the webhook scope.

secret.description: Secret used to generate payload signature.

status.description:
Status of the webhook. Use `active` to reactivate a disabled webhook.
 * `active` - Webhook is active.
 * `inactive` - Webhook is inactive.

created.description: Date and time the webhook was created.

ownedBy.description: Specify `org` when creating an org/admin level webhook. Supported for `meetings`, `recordings`, `convergedRecordings`, `meetingParticipants`, `meetingTranscripts`, `videoMeshAlerts`, `controlHubAlerts`, `rooms`, `messaging` and `adminBatchJobs`  (for Compliance Officers and messages with file attachments only - see [inline file DLP](/docs/api/guides/webex-real-time-file-dlp-basics)) resources.

locationId.description: The ID to use for this location in the [/locations API](/docs/api/v1/locations)

displayName.description: A friendly name for the location.

unit.description: Output data unit (only present if `metricName` is `temperature`).

items.description: The structure of the elements will depend on whether or not aggregated data was requested

start.description: Timestamp indicating the start of the aggregation bucket (ISO 8601).

end.description: Timestamp indicating the end of the aggregation bucket (ISO 8601).

duration.description: The time duration (in a given state) in the bucket.

orgId.description: `OrgId` associated with the workspace.

locationId.description: Location associated with the workspace (ID to use for the [/locations API](/docs/api/v1/locations)).

workspaceLocationId.description: Legacy workspace location ID associated with the workspace. Prefer `locationId`.

displayName.description: A friendly name for the workspace.

capacity.description: How many people the workspace is suitable for.

type.description:
The workspace type.
 * `notSet` - No workspace type set.
 * `focus` - High concentration.
 * `huddle` - Brainstorm/collaboration.
 * `meetingRoom` - Dedicated meeting space.
 * `open` - Unstructured agile.
 * `desk` - Individual.
 * `other` - Unspecified.

sipAddress.description: `SipUrl` to call all the devices associated with the workspace.

created.description: The date and time that the workspace was registered, in ISO8601 format.

type.description:
Calling.
 * `freeCalling` - Free Calling.
 * `hybridCalling` - Hybrid Calling.
 * `webexCalling` - Webex Calling.
 * `webexEdgeForDevices` - Webex Edge For Devices.
 * `thirdPartySipCalling` - Third-party SIP URI.
 * `none` - No Calling.

emailAddress.description: End user email address in Cisco Unified CM.

hybridCalling.description: The `hybridCalling` object only applies when calling type is `hybridCalling`.

licenses.description: The Webex Calling license associated with this workspace.

webexCalling.description: The `webexCalling` object only applies when calling type is `webexCalling`.

type.description:
* `none` - No calendar.
 * `google` - Google Calendar.
 * `microsoft` - Microsoft Exchange or Office 365.

emailAddress.description: Workspace email address. Will not be set when the calendar type is `none`.

calendar.description: Calendar type. Calendar of type `none` does not include an `emailAddress` field.

hotdeskingStatus.description:
Hot desking status of the workspace.
 * `on` - Workspace supports hotdesking.
 * `off` - Workspace does not support hotdesking.

supportedDevices.description:
The supported devices for the workspace. Default is `collaborationDevices`.
 * `collaborationDevices` - Workspace supports collaboration devices.
 * `phones` - Workspace supports MPP phones.

enabled.description: `true` if enabled or `false` otherwise.

siteUrl.description: The Webex site for the device hosting meetings.

devicePlatform.description:
The device platform.
 * `cisco` - Cisco.
 * `microsoftTeamsRoom` - Microsoft Teams Room.

url.description: URL of a map locating the workspace.

level.description: Health level. The level is based on the list of issues associated with the workspace.

devices.description: A list of devices associated with the workspace.

displayName.description: A friendly name for the workspace.

orgId.description: `OrgId` associated with the workspace. Only admin users of another organization (such as partners) may use this parameter.

locationId.description: Location associated with the workspace. Must be provided when the `floorId` is set.

workspaceLocationId.description: Legacy workspace location ID associated with the workspace. Prefer `locationId`.

capacity.description: How many people the workspace is suitable for. If set, must be 0 or higher.

type.description:
The type that best describes the workspace.
 * `notSet` - No workspace type set.
 * `focus` - High concentration.
 * `huddle` - Brainstorm/collaboration.
 * `meetingRoom` - Dedicated meeting space.
 * `open` - Unstructured agile.
 * `desk` - Individual.
 * `other` - Unspecified.

sipAddress.description: The `sipAddress` field can only be provided when calling type is `thirdPartySipCalling`.

type.description:
The calling type that is supported on the workspace.
 * `freeCalling` - Free Calling (Default).
 * `hybridCalling` - Hybrid Calling.
 * `webexCalling` - Webex Calling.
 * `webexEdgeForDevices` - Webex Edge For Devices.
 * `thirdPartySipCalling` - Third-party SIP URI.
 * `none` - No Calling.

licenses.description: A list of Webex Calling License IDs. If multiple license IDs are provided, the oldest suitable one will be applied.

webexCalling.description: The `webexCalling` object only applies when calling type is `webexCalling`.

emailAddress.description: Workspace email address. Will not be set when the calendar type is `none`.

resourceGroupId.description: The `ResourceGroup` ID is associated with the workspace and is only applicable for on-premise MICROSOFT calendar.

calendar.description: Workspace calendar configuration requires a `type` (`microsoft`, `google`, or `none`), an `emailAddress`, and a `resourceGroupId`. The default is `none`.

hotdeskingStatus.description:
Hot desking status of the workspace.
 * `on` - Workspace supports hotdesking.
 * `off` - Workspace does not support hotdesking.

enabled.description: Set to `true` to enable or `false` to disable.

siteUrl.description: The Webex site for device hosting meetings.

deviceHostedMeetings.description: To enable device hosted meetings, set a Webex `siteUrl` and the `enabled` flag to `true`.

supportedDevices.description:
The supported devices for the workspace. Default is `collaborationDevices`.
 * `collaborationDevices` - Workspace supports collaboration devices.
 * `phones` - Workspace supports MPP phones.

url.description: URL of a map locating the workspace.

displayName.description: A friendly name for the workspace.

locationId.description: Location associated with the workspace. Must be provided when the `floorId` is set.

workspaceLocationId.description: Legacy workspace location ID associated with the workspace. Prefer `locationId`.

capacity.description: How many people the workspace is suitable for. If set, must be 0 or higher.

type.description:
The type that best describes the workspace.
 * `notSet` - No workspace type set.
 * `focus` - High concentration.
 * `huddle` - Brainstorm/collaboration.
 * `meetingRoom` - Dedicated meeting space.
 * `open` - Unstructured agile.
 * `desk` - Individual.
 * `other` - Unspecified.

emailAddress.description: Workspace email address. Will not be set when the calendar type is `none`.

resourceGroupId.description: The `ResourceGroup` ID is associated with the workspace and is only applicable for on-premise MICROSOFT calendar.

calendar.description: An empty or null `calendar` field will not cause any changes. Provide a `type` (`microsoft`, `google`, or `none`), an `emailAddress`, and a `resourceGroupId`. To remove a calendar, set the `type` to `none`; this does not require an `emailAddress` or `resourceGroupId`.

sipAddress.description: The `sipAddress` field can only be provided when calling type is `thirdPartySipCalling`.

type.description:
The calling type that is supported on the workspace.
 * `freeCalling` - Free Calling (Default).
 * `hybridCalling` - Hybrid Calling.
 * `webexCalling` - Webex Calling.
 * `webexEdgeForDevices` - Webex Edge For Devices.
 * `thirdPartySipCalling` - Third-party SIP URI.
 * `none` - No Calling.

calling.description: Calling types supported on update are `freeCalling`, `thirdPartySipCalling`, `webexCalling` and `none`.

hotdeskingStatus.description:
Hot desking status of the workspace.
 * `on` - Workspace supports hotdesking.
 * `off` - Workspace does not support hotdesking.

deviceHostedMeetings.description: To enable device hosted meetings, set a Webex `siteUrl` and the `enabled` flag to `true`.

url.description: URL of a map locating the workspace. Passing `null` will remove the configuration.

supported.description: Is the workspace capability supported or not.

configured.description: Is the workspace capability configured or not.

SupportAndConfiguredInfo.description: Support and configured information for a workspace capability.

id.description: A unique identifier for the chat snippet.

chatTime.description: Chat time for the chat snippet in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

text.description: The text of the chat snippet.

meetingId.description: A unique identifier for the [meeting instance](/docs/meetings#meeting-series-scheduled-meetings-and-meeting-instances) to which the chat belongs.

type.description: Whether the type of the chat is private, public or group. Private chat is for the 1:1 chat. Public chat is for the message which is sent to all the people in the meeting. Group chat is for the message which is sent to a small group of people, like a message to "host and presenter".

email.description: Email address of the sender of the meeting chat snippet.

displayName.description: Display name for the sender.

personId.description: A unique identifier for the sender.

orgId.description: The ID of the organization to which the sender belongs.

sender.description: Information of the sender of the chat snippet.

email.description: Email address of the receiver of the meeting chat snippet.

displayName.description: Display name for the receiver.

personId.description: A unique identifier for the receiver.

orgId.description: The ID of the organization to which the receiver belongs.

receivers.description: Information of the receivers of the chat snippet.

id.description: A unique identifier for the closed caption.

meetingId.description: Unique identifier for the [meeting instance](/docs/meetings#meeting-series-scheduled-meetings-and-meeting-instances) which the closed captions belong to.

vttDownloadLink.description: The download link for the closed caption vtt file.

txtDownloadLink.description: The download link for the closed caption txt file.

start.description: Start time for the meeting closed caption in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

id.description: A unique identifier for the snippet.

personName.description: Name of the person generating the speech for the snippet.

personEmail.description: Email address of the person generating the speech for the snippet.

offsetMillisecond.description: Offset from the beginning of the parent transcript in milliseconds indicating the start time of the snippet.

durationMillisecond.description: Duration of the snippet in milliseconds.

email.description: Email address for meeting invitee.

displayName.description:
Display name for meeting invitee. The maximum length of `displayName` is 128 characters. In the Webex app, if the email has been associated with an existing Webex account, the display name associated with the Webex account will be used; otherwise, the `email` will be used as `displayName`. In a Webex site, if `displayName` is specified, it will show `displayName`. If `displayName` is not specified, and the `email` has been associated with an existing Webex account, the display name associated with the Webex account will be used; otherwise, the `email` will be used as `displayName`.

If the invitee has an existing Webex account, the `displayName` shown in the meeting will be the `displayName` associated with the Webex account; otherwise, `displayName` shown in the meeting will be the `displayName` which is specified by the invitee who does not have a Webex account.

coHost.description: Whether or not the invitee is a designated alternate host for the meeting. See [Add Alternate Hosts for Cisco Webex Meetings](https://help.webex.com/b5z6he/) for more details.

hostEmail.description: Email address for the meeting host. This attribute should only be set if the user or application calling the API has the admin on-behalf-of scopes. When used, the admin may specify the email of a user in a site they manage to be the meeting host.

sendEmail.description: If `true`, send an email to the invitee.

panelist.description: If `true`, the invitee is a designated panelist for the event meeting.

meetingId.description: Unique identifier for the meeting to which a person is being invited. This attribute only applies to meeting series and scheduled meeting. If it's a meeting series, the meeting invitee is invited to the entire meeting series; if it's a scheduled meeting, the meeting invitee is invited to this individual scheduled meeting. It doesn't apply to an ended or ongoing meeting instance. The meeting ID of a scheduled [personal room](https://help.webex.com/en-us/article/nul0wut/Webex-Personal-Rooms-in-Webex-Meetings) meeting is not supported for this API.

email.description: Email address for meeting invitee.

displayName.description:
Display name for meeting invitee. The maximum length of `displayName` is 128 characters. In the Webex app, if the email has been associated with an existing Webex account, the display name associated with the Webex account will be used; otherwise, the `email` will be used as `displayName`. In a Webex site, if `displayName` is specified, it will show `displayName`. If `displayName` is not specified, and the `email` has been associated with an existing Webex account, the display name associated with the Webex account will be used; otherwise, the `email` will be used as `displayName`.

If the invitee has an existing Webex account, the `displayName` shown in the meeting will be the `displayName` associated with the Webex account; otherwise, `displayName` shown in the meeting will be the `displayName` which is specified by the invitee who does not have a Webex account.

coHost.description: Whether or not the invitee is a designated alternate host for the meeting. See [Add Alternate Hosts for Cisco Webex Meetings](https://help.webex.com/b5z6he/) for more details.

hostEmail.description: Email address for the meeting host. This attribute should only be set if the user or application calling the API has the admin on-behalf-of scopes. When used, the admin may specify the email of a user in a site they manage to be the meeting host.

sendEmail.description: If `true`, send an email to the invitee.

panelist.description: If `true`, the invitee is a designated panelist for the event meeting.

panelist.description: If `true`, the invitee is a designated panelist for the event meeting.

In Webex App, if the email has been associated with an existing Webex account, the display name associated with the Webex account will be used; otherwise, the `email` will be used as `displayName`. In Webex site, if `displayName` is specified, it will show `displayName`. If `displayName` is not specified, and the `email` has been associated with an existing Webex account, the display name associated with the Webex account will be used; otherwise, the `email` will be used as `displayName`.

id.description: Unique identifier for the breakout session the participant attended.

name.description: Name of the breakout session the participant attended.

joinedTime.description: The time the participant joined the breakout session.

leftTime.description: The time the participant left the breakout session.

id.description: The ID that identifies the meeting and the participant.

orgId.description: The ID that identifies the organization. It only applies to participants of ongoing meetings.

host.description: Whether or not the participant is the host of the meeting.

coHost.description: Whether or not the participant has host privilege in the meeting.

spaceModerator.description: Whether or not the participant is the team space moderator. This field returns only if the meeting is associated with a Webex space.

email.description: The email address of the participant.

displayName.description: The name of the participant.

invitee.description: Whether or not the participant is invited to the meeting.

muted.description: Whether or not the participant's audio is muted.

video.description:
The status of the participant's video.
 * `on` - The video is turned on.
 * `off` - The video is turned off.

state.description:
The status of the participant in the meeting. The value of `state` is `breakoutSession` which is only returned when the meeting is in progress and the breakout session is enabled.
 * `lobby` - The participant is waiting in the meeting lobby.
 * `end` - The participant has left the meeting.
 * `joined` - The participant has joined the meeting and is in the main session.
 * `breakoutSession` - The participant has joined a breakout session.

breakoutSessionId.description: The ID of the breakout session including the participant.

joinedTime.description: The time the participant joined the meeting. If the field is non-existent or shows `1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z` the meeting may be still ongoing and the `joinedTime` will be filled in after the meeting ended. If you need real-time join events, please refer to the webhooks guide.

leftTime.description: The time the participant left the meeting. If the field is non-existent or shows `1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z` the meeting may be still ongoing and the `leftTime` will be filled in after the meeting ended. If you need real-time left events, please refer to the webhooks guide.

siteUrl.description: The site URL.

meetingId.description: A unique identifier for the meeting which the participant belongs to.

hostEmail.description: The email address of the host.

breakoutSessionsAttended.description: The breakout sessions attended by the participant. Only applies to ended meeting instances.

sourceId.description: The source ID of the participant. The `sourceId` is from the [Create Invitation Sources](/docs/api/v1/meetings/create-invitation-sources) API.

id.description: The participant ID that identifies the meeting and the participant.

orgId.description: The ID that identifies the organization.

host.description: Whether or not the participant is the host of the meeting.

coHost.description: Whether or not the participant has host privilege in the meeting.

spaceModerator.description: Whether or not the participant is the team space moderator. This field returns only if the meeting is associated with a Webex space.

email.description: The email address of the participant.

displayName.description: The name of the participant.

invitee.description: Whether or not the participant is invited to the meeting.

video.description:
The status of the participant's video.
 * `on` - The video is turned on.
 * `off` - The video is turned off.

muted.description: Whether or not the participant's audio is muted.

state.description:
The status of the participant in the meeting.
 * `lobby` - The participant is waiting in the meeting lobby.
 * `joined` - The participant has joined the meeting.

siteUrl.description: The site URL.

meetingId.description: A unique identifier for the meeting which the participant belongs to.

hostEmail.description: The email address of the host.

correlationId.description: An internal ID that is associated with each join.

audioType.description:
The audio type that the participant is using.
 * `pstn` - `PSTN`
 * `voip` - `VoIP`
 * `inactive` - The participant is not connected to audio.

joinedTime.description: The time the device joined the meeting. If the field is non-existent or shows `1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z` the meeting may be still ongoing and the `joinedTime` will be filled in after the meeting ended. If you need real-time joined events, please refer to the webhooks guide.

leftTime.description: The time the device left the meeting, `leftTime` is the exact moment when a specific device left the meeting. If the field is non-existent or shows `1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z` the meeting may be still ongoing and the `leftTime` will be filled in after the meeting ended. If you need real-time left events, please refer to the webhooks guide.

participantId.description: The ID that identifies the meeting participant.

Type of the `address`. The default value is `sipAddress`.
 * `sipAddress` - SIP address.

invitationCorrelationId.description: An internal ID that is associated with the call-out invitation. Only UUIDs with hyphens are supported. The letters in the UUID must be in lowercase. A random UUID will be generated automatically if not specified.

displayName.description: The display name of the invited SIP participant. The maximum length is 32 characters.

participantId.description: Participant ID. It can be used in the "Cancel Call Out a SIP Participant" API.

address.description: SIP address of the invited SIP participant.

displayName.description: The display name of the invited SIP participant.

state.description:
The state of the invited SIP participant.
 * `pending` - The invited SIP participant is waiting for approval. Participants in the `pending` state will not be listed by the "List Meeting Participants" API.

participantId.description: ID of the SIP participant on whom the callout is to be cancelled. It can be retrieved from the response of the "Call Out a SIP Participant" API.
The ID that identifies the meeting participant.

breakoutSessionId.description: The breakout session ID that identifies which breakout session to admit the participant into. Admit into the main session if the value is empty.

endTime.description: The date and time the poll ended in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

totalRespondents.description: The total number of respondents in the poll.

endTime.description: The date and time the poll ended in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

topic.description: Personal Meeting Room topic. The length of `topic` must be between 1 and 128 characters. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

hostPin.description: PIN for joining the room as host. The host PIN must be digits of a predefined length, e.g. 4 digits. It cannot contain sequential digits, such as 1234 or 4321, or repeated digits of the predefined length, such as 1111. The predefined length for host PIN can be viewed in user's `My Personal Room` page. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

personalMeetingRoomLink.description: Personal Meeting Room link. It cannot be empty. ***Note***: This is a read-only attribute.

enabledAutoLock.description: Option to automatically lock the Personal Room a number of minutes after a meeting starts. When a room is locked, invitees cannot enter until the owner admits them. The period after which the meeting is locked is defined by `autoLockMinutes`. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

autoLockMinutes.description: Number of minutes after which the Personal Room is locked if `enabledAutoLock` is enabled. Valid options are 0, 5, 10, 15 and 20. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

enabledNotifyHost.description: Flag to enable notifying the owner of a Personal Room when someone enters the Personal Room lobby while the owner is not in the room. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

supportCoHost.description: Flag allowing other invitees to host a meeting in the Personal Room without the owner. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

supportAnyoneAsCoHost.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee with a host account on the target site to become a cohost when joining the Personal Room. The target site is user's preferred site. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

allowFirstUserToBeCoHost.description: Whether or not to allow the first attendee with a host account on the target site to become a cohost when joining the Personal Room. The target site is user's preferred site. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

allowAuthenticatedDevices.description: Whether or not to allow authenticated video devices in the user's organization to start or join the meeting without a prompt. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

email.description: Email address for cohost. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

displayName.description: Display name for cohost. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

coHosts.description: Array defining cohosts for the room if both `supportAnyoneAsCoHost` and `allowFirstUserToBeCoHost` are `false` This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

sipAddress.description: SIP address for callback from a video system.

dialInIpAddress.description: IP address for callback from a video system.

accessCode.description: Code for authenticating a user to join teleconference. Users join the teleconference using the call-in number or the global call-in number, followed by the value of the `accessCode`.

callInNumber.description: Call-in number to join teleconference from a phone.

tollType.description: Type of toll for the call-in number.

callInNumbers.description: Array of call-in numbers for joining teleconference from a phone.

rel.description: Link relation describing how the target resource is related to the current context (conforming with [RFC5998](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988)).

links.description: [HATEOAS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HATEOAS) information of global call-in numbers for joining teleconference from a phone.

telephony.description: Information for callbacks from meeting to phone or for joining a teleconference using a phone.

defaultAudioType.description:
Default audio type. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.
 * `webexAudio` - Webex audio. This supports telephony and VoIP.
 * `voipOnly` - Support only VoIP.
 * `otherTeleconferenceService` - Other teleconference service. Details are defined in the `otherTeleconferenceDescription` parameter.
 * `none` - No audio.

otherTeleconferenceDescription.description: Phone number and other information for the teleconference provider to be used, along with instructions for invitees. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

enabledGlobalCallIn.description: Flag to enable/disable global call ins. ***Note***: If the site does not support global call-ins, you cannot set this option. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

enabledTollFree.description: Flag to enable/disable call-ins from toll-free numbers.  ***Note***: If the site does not support calls from toll-free numbers, you cannot set this option. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

enabledAutoConnection.description: Flag to enable/disable automatically connecting to audio using a computer. The meeting host can enable/disable this option. When this option is set to `true`, the user is automatically connected to audio via a computer when they start or join a Webex Meetings meeting on a desktop. `This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

audioPin.description: PIN to provide a secondary level of authentication for calls where the host is using the phone and may need to invite additional invitees. It must be exactly 4 digits. It cannot contain sequential digits, such as 1234 or 4321, or repeat a digit 4 times, such as 1111. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

countryCode.description: Country code for the phone number. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

number.description: Phone number. It cannot be longer than 30 characters. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

enabledCallInAuthentication.description: Flag identifying the phone number as the one that will be used to dial into a teleconference. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

enabledCallMe.description: Flag to enable/disable Call Me number display on the meeting client. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API. ***Note***: This feature is only effective if the site supports the ***Call Me*** feature.

officeNumber.description: Office phone number. We recommend that phone numbers be specified to facilitate connecting via audio. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

countryCode.description: Country code for the phone number. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

number.description: Phone number. It cannot be longer than 30 characters. This attribute can be modified with the with the [Update Audio Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-audio-options) API.

enabledCallMe.description: Flag to enable/disable Call Me number display on the meeting client. deviceAddress.description: Video address. It cannot be empty and must be in valid email format. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Video Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-video-options) API.

emails.description: You can allow other hosts to schedule meetings on your behalf by entering their email addresses here. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Scheduling Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-scheduling-options), [Insert Delegate Emails](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/insert-delegate-emails), and [Update Scheduling Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/delete-delegate-emails) APIs.

default.description: Flag identifying the site as the default site. Users can list meetings and recordings, and create meetings on the default site.

sites.description: Array of sites for the user. Users can have one site or multiple sites. This concept is specific to Webex Meetings. Any `siteUrl` in the site list can be assigned as user's default site with the [Update Default Site](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-default-site) API.

siteUrl.description: Access URL for the site. ***Note***: This is a read-only attribute. The value can be assigned as user's default site with the [Update Default Site](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-default-site) API.

default.description: Flag identifying the site as the default site. Users can list meetings and recordings, and create meetings on the default site.

siteUrl.description: Access URL for the site.

topic.description: Personal Meeting Room topic to be updated.

hostPin.description: Updated PIN for joining the room as host. The host PIN must be digits of a predefined length, e.g. 4 digits. It cannot contain sequential digits, such as 1234 or 4321, or repeated digits of the predefined length, such as 1111. The predefined length for host PIN can be viewed in user's `My Personal Room` page and it can only be changed by site administrator.

enabledAutoLock.description: Update for option to automatically lock the Personal Room a number of minutes after a meeting starts. When a room is locked, invitees cannot enter until the owner admits them. The period after which the meeting is locked is defined by `autoLockMinutes`.

autoLockMinutes.description: Updated number of minutes after which the Personal Room is locked if `enabledAutoLock` is enabled. Valid options are 0, 5, 10, 15 and 20.

enabledNotifyHost.description: Update for flag to enable notifying the owner of a Personal Room when someone enters the Personal Room lobby while the owner is not in the room.

supportCoHost.description: Update for flag allowing other invitees to host a meetingCoHost in the Personal Room without the owner.

supportAnyoneAsCoHost.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee with a host account on the target site to become a cohost when joining the Personal Room. The target site is user's preferred site.

allowFirstUserToBeCoHost.description: Whether or not to allow the first attendee with a host account on the target site to become a cohost when joining the Personal Room. The target site is user's preferred site.

allowAuthenticatedDevices.description: Whether or not to allow authenticated video devices in the user's organization to start or join the meeting without a prompt.

email.description: Email address for cohost. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

displayName.description: Display name for cohost. This attribute can be modified with the [Update Personal Meeting Room Options](/docs/api/v1/meeting-preferences/update-personal-meeting-room-options) API.

coHosts.description: Updated array defining cohosts for the room if both `supportAnyoneAsCoHost` and `allowFirstUserToBeCoHost` are `false`

siteUrl.description: Site URL to refresh the personal room IDs.

personalMeetingRoomIds.description: Information of whose personal room IDs are to be refreshed and how to refresh. The maximum size of `items` is 100.

email.description: Email address of the meeting host whose personal room ID will be refreshed.

personId.description: Unique identifier for the meeting host whose personal room ID will be refreshed.

systemGenerated.description: Whether or not to automatically refresh the personal room ID by the site's settings. Refresh the personal room ID by the site's settings automatically if `systemGenerated` is true; otherwise, replace the existing personal room ID with the specified value if `systemGenerated` is false or not specified.

personalMeetingRoomId.description: New personal room ID specified by the admin user.

siteUrl.description: URL of the Webex site.

personalMeetingRoomIds.description: Information for the refreshed personal room IDs.

email.description: Email address for the meeting host whose personal room ID has been refreshed.

personId.description: Unique identifier for the meeting host whose personal room ID has been refreshed.

meetingId.description: A unique identifier for the [meeting instance](/docs/meetings#meeting-series-scheduled-meetings-and-meeting-instances) to which the Q&A belongs.

totalAttendees.description: The total number of attendees in the meeting.

totalRespondents.description: The total number of respondents in the meeting.

displayName.description: The name of the user who asked the question.

email.description: The email of the user who asked the question.

prev.description: Link to the previous page of answers to this question.

self.description: Link to the current page of this answers to this question.

next.description: Link to the next page of answers to this question.

links.description: The pagination links of the question's answers.

items.description: An array of answer objects for this question.

displayName.description: The name of the person who answered the question.

email.description: The email of the person who answered the question.

personId.description: The ID of the person who answered the question. Only present for authenticated users.

answered.description: Whether or not the question was answered.

id.description: The ID of the session type.

shortName.description: The short name of the session type.

siteUrl.description: Site URL for the session type.

name.description: The name of the session type.

The meeting type of meeting that you can create with the session type.
 * `meeting` - Meeting Center.
 * `webinar` - Webinar meeting.
 * `privateMeeting` - Private meeting.
 * `EventCenter` - Event Center.
 * `SupportCenter` - Support Center.
 * `TrainCenter` - Training Center.

personId.description: A unique identifier for the user.

email.description: The email of the user.

siteUrl.description: Site URL for the user.

sessionTypes.description: All session types are supported by the user on the site.

id.description: The ID of the session type.

shortName.description: The short name of the session type.

name.description: The name of the session type.

type.description:
The meeting type of meeting that you can create with the session type.
 * `meeting` - Meeting Center.
 * `webinar` - Webinar meeting.
 * `privateMeeting` - Private meeting.
 * `EventCenter` - Event Center.
 * `SupportCenter` - Support Center.
 * `TrainCenter` - Training Center.

siteUrl.description: Site URL for the session type.

personId.description: A unique identifier for the user.

email.description: The email of the user.

sessionTypeIds.description: An array of the session type ID.

id.description: A unique identifier for the transcript.

siteUrl.description: URL of the Webex site from which the API lists meeting transcripts.

startTime.description: Start time for the meeting transcript in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format.

meetingId.description: Unique identifier for the [meeting instance](/docs/meetings#meeting-series-scheduled-meetings-and-meeting-instances) to which the transcripts belong.

scheduledMeetingId.description: Unique identifier for scheduled meeting with which the current meeting is associated. Only apples to a meeting instance which is happening or has happened. This is the `id` of the scheduled meeting with which the instance is associated.

meetingSeriesId.description: Unique identifier for the parent meeting series to which the recording belongs.

hostUserId.description: Unique identifier for the meeting host.

vttDownloadLink.description: The download link for the transcript vtt file.

txtDownloadLink.description: The download link for the transcript txt file.

status.description:
* `available` - Transcript is available.
 * `deleted` - Transcript has been deleted.

reason.description: Reason for snippet update; only required for Compliance Officers.
Reason for deleting a transcript. Only required when a Compliance Officer is operating on another user's transcript.

comment.description: Explanation for deleting a transcript. The comment can be a maximum of 255 characters long.

meetingId.description: Unique identifier for the meeting.

start.description: The date and time when the meeting was started. It's in the timezone specified in the request header or in the `UTC` timezone if timezone is not specified.

end.description: The date and time when the meeting was ended. It's in the timezone specified in the request header or in the `UTC` timezone if timezone is not specified.

duration.description: Duration of the meeting in minutes.

scheduledType.description:
Scheduled type for the meeting.
 * `meeting` - Regular meeting.
 * `webinar` - Webinar meeting.

hostDisplayName.description: Display name for the meeting host.

hostEmail.description: Email address for the meeting host.

totalCallOutDomestic.description: Aggregated attendee domestic PSTN call-out minutes.

totalCallInTollFreeMinutes.description: Aggregated attendee toll-free PSTN call-in minutes.

totalCallOutInternational.description: Aggregated attendee international PSTN call-out minutes.

totalParticipants.description: Total number of participants of the meeting.

totalParticipantsVoip.description: Total number of VoIP participants of the meeting.

totalParticipantsCallIn.description: Total number of PSTN call-in participants of the meeting.

totalParticipantsCallOut.description: Total number of PSTN call-out participants of the meeting.

peakAttendee.description: Peak number of attendees throughout the meeting.

totalRegistered.description: Total number of registrants of the meeting.

totalInvitee.description: Total number of invitees of the meeting.

serviceType.description:
The service type for the meeting usage report.
 * `MeetingCenter` - The service type for the usage report is meeting.
 * `EventCenter` - The service type for the usage report is the event.
 * `TrainingCenter` - The service type for the usage report is the training session.
 * `SupportCenter` - The service type for the usage report is the support meeting.

name.description: Name of the tracking code.

value.description: Value of the tracking code.

meetingId.description: Unique identifier for the meeting.

displayName.description: Attendee's display name.

email.description: Attendee's email.

joinedTime.description: The date and time when the attendee joined the meeting. It's in the timezone specified in the request header or in the `UTC` timezone if timezone is not specified.

leftTime.description: The date and time when the attendee left the meeting. It's in the timezone specified in the request header or in the `UTC` timezone if timezone is not specified.

duration.description: Duration of the attendee in the meeting in minutes.

participantType.description:
The attendee's role in the meeting.
 * `host` - Meeting host.
 * `attendee` - Meeting attendee.

ipAddress.description: IP address of the attendee when he attended the meeting.

clientAgent.description: Information of the attendee's operating system and application when he attended the meeting.

registered.description: Whether or not the attendee has registered the meeting.

invited.description: Whether or not the attendee has been invited to the meeting.

email.description: Email address of meeting invitee.

displayName.description: Display name of meeting invitee. The maximum length of `displayName` is 128 characters. If not specified but the email has been registered, user's registered name for the email will be taken as `displayName`. If not specified and the email hasn't been registered, the email will be taken as `displayName`.

coHost.description: Whether or not invitee is allowed to be a cohost for the meeting. `coHost` for each invitee is `true` by default if `roomId` is specified when creating a meeting, and anyone in the invitee list that is not qualified to be a cohost will be invited as a non-cohost invitee.

panelist.description: Whether or not an invitee is allowed to be a panelist. Only applies to webinars.

languageCode1.description: Forms a set of simultaneous interpretation channels together with `languageCode2`. Standard language format from [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) code. Read [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) for details.

languageCode2.description: Forms a set of simultaneous interpretation channels together with `languageCode1`. Standard language format from [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) code. Read [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) for details.

email.description: Email address of meeting interpreter.

displayName.description: Display name of meeting interpreter.

languageCode1.description: Forms a set of simultaneous interpretation channels together with `languageCode2`. Standard language format from [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) code. Read [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) for details.

languageCode2.description: Forms a set of simultaneous interpretation channels together with `languageCode1`. Standard language format from [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) code. Read [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) for details.

email.description: Email address of meeting interpreter.

displayName.description: Display name of meeting interpreter.

id.description: Unique identifier for meeting. For a meeting series, the `id` is used to identify the entire series. For scheduled meetings from a series, the `id` is used to identify that scheduled meeting. For a meeting instance that is in progress or has concluded, the `id` is used to identify that instance.

meetingNumber.description: Meeting number. Applies to meeting series, scheduled meeting, and meeting instances, but not to meeting instances which have ended.

title.description: Meeting title. Can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

agenda.description: Meeting agenda. The agenda can be a maximum of 1300 characters long. This attribute can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

password.description: Meeting password. Applies to meeting series, scheduled meetings, and in-progress meeting instances, but not to meeting instances which have ended. Can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

phoneAndVideoSystemPassword.description: 8-digit numeric password used to join a meeting from audio and video devices. This attribute applies to meeting series, scheduled meetings, and in-progress meeting instances, but not to meeting instances which have ended.

meetingType.description:
Meeting type.
 * `meetingSeries` - Primary instance of a scheduled series of meetings which consists of one or more scheduled meetings based on a `recurrence` rule. When a non-recurring meeting is scheduled with no `recurrence`, its `meetingType` is also `meetingSeries` which is a meeting series with only one occurrence in Webex meeting modeling.
 * `scheduledMeeting` - Instance from a primary meeting series.
 * `meeting` - Meeting instance that is in progress or has completed.

state.description:
Meeting state.
 * `active` - Only applies to a meeting series. Indicates that one or more future scheduled meetings exist for this meeting series.
 * `scheduled` - Only applies to scheduled meeting. Indicates that the meeting is scheduled in the future.
 * `ready` - Only applies to scheduled meeting. Indicates that this scheduled meeting is ready to start or join immediately.
 * `lobby` - Only applies to meeting instances. Indicates that a locked meeting has been joined by participants, but no hosts have joined.
 * `inProgress` - Applies to meeting series and meeting instances. For a meeting series, indicates that an instance of this series is happening now. For a meeting instance, indicates that the meeting has been joined and unlocked.
 * `ended` - Applies to scheduled meetings and meeting instances. For scheduled meetings, indicates that the meeting was started and is now over. For meeting instances, indicates that the meeting instance has concluded.
 * `missed` - This state only applies to scheduled meetings. Indicates that the meeting was scheduled in the past but never happened.
 * `expired` - This state only applies to a meeting series. Indicates that all scheduled meetings of this series have passed.

hostDidJoin.description: Only applies to meeting series in the `inProgress` state, scheduled meetings in the `ready` state, and meeting instances in the `inProgress` state, whether or not the meeting host joined the meeting. If true, the meeting host has joined the meeitng even if they dropped off; otherwise, the meeting host hasn't ever join the meeting. Indicates that someone is waiting in the lobby and the host hasn't joined the meeting if `attendeeDidJoin` is true and `hostDidJoin` is false.

attendeeDidJoin.description: Only applies to meeting series in the `inProgress` state, scheduled meetings in the `ready` state, and meeting instances in the `inProgress` state. Whether or not anyone has joined the meeting. If true, the meeting host or any attendee has joined the meeting; otherwise, no one has joined the meeting. Indicates that someone is waiting in the lobby and the host hasn't joined the meeting if `attendeeDidJoin` is true and `hostDidJoin` is false.

timezone.description: [Time zone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones#List) of `start` and `end`, conforming with the [IANA time zone database](https://www.iana.org/time-zones).

start.description: Start time for meeting in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) compliant format. If the meeting is a meeting series, `start` is the date and time the first meeting of the series starts. If the meeting is a meeting series and the `current` filter is true, `start` is the date and time the upcoming or ongoing meeting of the series starts. If the meeting is a scheduled meeting from a meeting series, `start` is the date and time when that scheduled meeting starts. If the meeting is a meeting instance that has happened or is happening, `start` is the date and time that the instance actually starts. Can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

end.description: End time for a meeting in ISO 8601 compliant format. If the meeting is a meeting series, `end` is the date and time the first meeting of the series ends. If the meeting is a meeting series and the current filter is true, `end` is the date and time the upcoming or ongoing meeting of the series ends. If the meeting is a scheduled meeting from a meeting series, `end` is the date and time when that scheduled meeting ends. If the meeting is a meeting instance that has happened, `end` is the date and time that instance actually ends. If a meeting instance is in progress, `end` is not available. Can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

recurrence.description: Meeting series recurrence rule (conforming with [RFC 2445](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2445.txt)). Applies only to a recurring meeting series, not to a meeting series with only one scheduled meeting. Can be modified for a meeting series using the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API. Multiple days or dates for monthly or yearly `recurrence` rule are not supported, only the first day or date specified is taken. For example, "FREQ=MONTHLY;INTERVAL=1;COUNT=10;BYMONTHDAY=10,11,12" is not supported and it will be partially supported as "FREQ=MONTHLY;INTERVAL=1;COUNT=10;BYMONTHDAY=10". For a non-recurring meeting which has no `recurrence`, its `meetingType` is also `meetingSeries` which is a meeting series with only one occurrence in Webex meeting modeling.

hostUserId.description: Unique identifier for the meeting host.

hostDisplayName.description: Display name for the meeting host.

hostEmail.description: Email address for the meeting host.

hostKey.description: Key for joining the meeting as host.

siteUrl.description: Site URL for the meeting.

webLink.description: Link to a meeting information page where the meeting client is launched if the meeting is ready to start or join.

sipAddress.description: SIP address for callback from a video system.

dialInIpAddress.description: IP address for callback from a video system.

roomId.description: Room ID of the associated Webex space. Only applies to ad-hoc meetings and space meetings.

enabledAutoRecordMeeting.description: Whether or not meeting is recorded automatically. Can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

allowAnyUserToBeCoHost.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee with a host account on the target site to become a cohost when joining the meeting. The target site is specified by a `siteUrl` parameter when creating the meeting. If not specified, it's a user's preferred site. The `allowAnyUserToBeCoHost` attribute can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

enabledJoinBeforeHost.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to join the meeting before the host joins the meeting. The `enabledJoinBeforeHost` attribute can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

enableConnectAudioBeforeHost.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to connect to audio before the host joins the meeting. Only applicable if the `enabledJoinBeforeHost` attribute is set to `true`. The `enableConnectAudioBeforeHost` attribute can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

joinBeforeHostMinutes.description: Number of minutes an attendee can join the meeting before the meeting start time and the host joins. Only applicable if the `enabledJoinBeforeHost` attribute is set to true. The `joinBeforeHostMinutes` attribute can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API. Valid options for a meeting are `0`, `5`, `10`, and `15`, and valid options for a webinar are `0`, `15`, `30`, `45`, and `60`. The default is `0` if not specified.

excludePassword.description: Whether or not to exclude the meeting password from the email invitation.

publicMeeting.description: Whether or not to allow the meeting to be listed on the public calendar.

reminderTime.description: The number of minutes before the meeting begins, that an email reminder is sent to the host.

unlockedMeetingJoinSecurity.description:
Specifies how the people who aren't on the invite can join the unlocked meeting.
 * `allowJoin` - If the value of `unlockedMeetingJoinSecurity` attribute is `allowJoin`, people can join the unlocked meeting directly.
 * `allowJoinWithLobby` - If the value of `unlockedMeetingJoinSecurity` attribute is `allowJoinWithLobby`, people will wait in the lobby until the host admits them.
 * `blockFromJoin` - If the value of `unlockedMeetingJoinSecurity` attribute is `blockFromJoin`, people can't join the unlocked meeting.

sessionTypeId.description: Unique identifier for a meeting session type for the user. This attribute is required when scheduling a webinar meeting. All available meeting session types enabled for the user can be retrieved using the [List Meeting Session Types](/docs/api/v1/meetings/list-meeting-session-types) API.

scheduledType.description:
Specifies whether the meeting is a regular meeting, a webinar, or a meeting scheduled in the user's [personal room](https://help.webex.com/en-us/article/nul0wut/Webex-Personal-Rooms-in-Webex-Meetings).
 * `meeting` - If the value of `scheduledType` attribute is `meeting`, it is a regular meeting.
 * `webinar` - If the value of `scheduledType` attribute is `webinar`, it is a webinar meeting.
 * `personalRoomMeeting` - If the value of `scheduledType` attribute is `personalRoomMeeting`, it is a meeting scheduled in the user's [personal room](https://help.webex.com/en-us/article/nul0wut/Webex-Personal-Rooms-in-Webex-Meetings).

enabledWebcastView.description: Whether or not webcast view is enabled.

panelistPassword.description: Password for panelists of a webinar meeting. Must conform to the site's password complexity settings. Read [password management](https://help.webex.com/en-us/zrupm6/Manage-Security-Options-for-Your-Site-in-Webex-Site-Administration) for details. If not specified, a random password conforming to the site's password rules will be generated automatically.

phoneAndVideoSystemPanelistPassword.description: 8-digit numeric panelist password to join a webinar meeting from audio and video devices.

enableAutomaticLock.description: Whether or not to automatically lock the meeting after it starts.

automaticLockMinutes.description: The number of minutes after the meeting begins, for automatically locking it.

allowFirstUserToBeCoHost.description: Whether or not to allow the first attendee of the meeting with a host account on the target site to become a cohost. The target site is specified by the `siteUrl` parameter when creating the meeting. If not specified, it's a user's preferred site. The `allowFirstUserToBeCoHost` attribute can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting uisng the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

allowAuthenticatedDevices.description: Whether or not to allow authenticated video devices in the meeting's organization to start or join the meeting without a prompt. This attribute can be modified for a meeting series or a scheduled meeting using the  [Update a Meeting](/docs/api/v1/meetings/update-a-meeting) API.

accessCode.description: Code for authenticating a user to join teleconference. Users join the teleconference using the call-in number or the global call-in number, followed by the value of the `accessCode`.

callInNumber.description: Call-in number to join the teleconference from a phone.

tollType.description: Type of toll for the call-in number.

callInNumbers.description: Array of call-in numbers for joining a teleconference from a phone.

links.description: [HATEOAS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HATEOAS) information of global call-in numbers for joining a teleconference from a phone.

telephony.description: Information for callbacks from a meeting to phone or for joining a teleconference using a phone.

enabledChat.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to chat in the meeting. Also depends on the session type.

enabledVideo.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to have video in the meeting. Also depends on the session type.

enabledPolling.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to poll in the meeting. Can only be set `true` for a webinar. The value of this attribute depends on the session type for a meeting. Please contact your site admin if this attribute is not available. Not supported if the site has been migrated to the Webex Suite meeting platform.

enabledNote.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to take notes in the meeting. The value of this attribute also depends on the session type. Not supported if the site has been migrated to the Webex Suite meeting platform.

noteType.description:
Whether note taking is enabled. If the value of `enabledNote` is false, users cannot set this attribute and get the default value `allowAll`. Not supported if the site has been migrated to the Webex Suite meeting platform.
 * `allowAll` - If the value of `noteType` attribute is `allowAll`, all participants can take notes.
 * `allowOne` - If the value of `noteType` attribute is `allowOne`, only a single note taker is allowed.

enabledFileTransfer.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to transfer files in the meeting. The value of this attribute also depends on the session type.

enabledUCFRichMedia.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to share [Universal Communications Format](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/collaboration/training_center/wbs30/WebEx_BK_TE1FB6C1_00_training-center-frequently-asked-questions/WebEx_BK_TE1FB6C1_00_training-center-frequently-asked-questions_chapter_0110.pdf) media files in the meeting. The value of this attribute also depends on the sessionType. Not supported if the site has been migrated to the Webex Suite meeting platform.

enabledShareContent.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to share content in the meeting.

enabledSaveDocument.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to save shared documents, slides, or whiteboards when they are shared as files in the content viewer instead of in a window or application.

enabledPrintDocument.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to print shared documents, slides, or whiteboards when they are shared as files in the content viewer instead of in a window or application.

enabledAnnotate.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to annotate shared documents, slides, or whiteboards when they are shared as files in the content viewer instead of in a window or application.

enabledViewParticipantList.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to view participants.

enabledViewThumbnails.description: Whether or not to allow any attendee to see a small preview image of any page of shared documents or slides when they are shared as files in the content viewer instead of in a window or application.
